title: State attributes
description: State attributes control visibility, interaction, and opacity. Most are Boolean; `visibility` is tri-state.

data.name: alpha
data.summary: 0.0–1.0. Translucency. `alpha: 0` is visually hidden but still receives touches — use `visibility` to disable entirely.

data.name: canTap
data.summary: Boolean. When `false`, `onClick` handlers are not invoked. Use to disable interaction without visual change.

data.name: confirmationDialog
data.summary: If set to an object with `title` / `message` / `confirmText`, shows a native confirm dialog before the `onClick` handler fires.

data.name: enabled
data.description: Whether component is enabled (can be data binding)
data.summary: Boolean. When `false`, the component ignores user interaction and applies its disabled styling (dimmed / desaturated).

data.name: hidden
data.description: Whether component is hidden (can be data binding)
data.summary: Shorthand for `visibility: 'gone'`. Boolean-valued. Deprecated for new code — prefer explicit `visibility`.

data.name: opacity
data.description: Opacity (0-1), alias for alpha - can be data binding

data.name: touchDisabledState
data.summary: Disables touch when the component is in a specified state (e.g., while an animation runs).

data.name: touchEnabledViewIds
data.summary: Array of sibling IDs that remain tappable even when this view blocks touches (e.g., for a dimming overlay that should not block specific buttons).

data.name: visibility
data.description: View visibility state (can be data binding)
data.summary: `visible` / `invisible` / `gone`. `invisible` preserves space; `gone` removes from layout entirely.

values.description: Not rendered but occupies space (acts as an invisible placeholder).
Not rendered and does not occupy space. Siblings shift to fill.
